root_text.an1.616:1.1: “Amataṁ te, bhikkhave, na paribhuñjanti ye kāyagatāsatiṁ na paribhuñjanti.
root_text.an1.616:1.2: Amataṁ te, bhikkhave, paribhuñjanti ye kāyagatāsatiṁ paribhuñjantī”ti.
root_text.an1.617:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, aparibhuttaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati aparibhuttā.
root_text.an1.617:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, paribhuttaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati paribhuttā”ti.
root_text.an1.618:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, parihīnaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati parihīnā.
root_text.an1.618:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, aparihīnaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati aparihīnā”ti.
root_text.an1.619:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, viraddhaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati viraddhā.
root_text.an1.619:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, āraddhaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati āraddhā”ti.
root_text.an1.620:1.1: “Amataṁ te, bhikkhave, pamādiṁsu ye kāyagatāsatiṁ pamādiṁsu.
root_text.an1.620:1.2: Amataṁ te, bhikkhave, na pamādiṁsu ye kāyagatāsatiṁ na pamādiṁsu”.
root_text.an1.621:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, pamuṭṭhaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati pamuṭṭhā.
root_text.an1.621:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, appamuṭṭhaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati appamuṭṭhā”ti.
root_text.an1.622:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, anāsevitaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati anāsevitā.
root_text.an1.622:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, āsevitaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati āsevitā”ti.
root_text.an1.623:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, abhāvitaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati abhāvitā.
root_text.an1.623:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvitaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati bhāvitā”ti.
root_text.an1.624:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, abahulīkataṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati abahulīkatā.
root_text.an1.624:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, bahulīkataṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati bahulīkatā”ti.
root_text.an1.625:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhiññātaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati anabhiññātā.
root_text.an1.625:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, abhiññātaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati abhiññātā”ti.
root_text.an1.626:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, apariññātaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati apariññātā.
root_text.an1.626:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, pariññātaṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati pariññātā”ti.
root_text.an1.627:1.1: “Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, asacchikataṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati asacchikatā.
root_text.an1.627:1.2: Amataṁ tesaṁ, bhikkhave, sacchikataṁ yesaṁ kāyagatāsati sacchikatā”ti.
root_text.an1.627:2.2: Attamanā te bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.

translation_text.an1.616:0.2: The Chapter on Freedom From Death
translation_text.an1.616:1.1: “Mendicants, those who don’t enjoy mindfulness of the body don’t enjoy freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.616:1.2: Those who enjoy mindfulness of the body enjoy freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.617:1.1: “Mendicants, those who haven’t enjoyed mindfulness of the body haven’t enjoyed freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.617:1.2: Those who have enjoyed mindfulness of the body have enjoyed freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.618:1.1: “Mendicants, those who have lost mindfulness of the body have lost freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.618:1.2: Those who haven’t lost mindfulness of the body haven’t lost freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.619:1.1: “Mendicants, those who have missed out on mindfulness of the body have missed out on freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.619:1.2: Those who have undertaken mindfulness of the body have not missed out on freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.620:1.1: “Mendicants, those who have neglected mindfulness of the body have neglected freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.620:1.2: Those who have not neglected mindfulness of the body have not neglected freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.621:1.1: “Mendicants, those who have forgotten mindfulness of the body have forgotten freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.621:1.2: Those who haven’t forgotten mindfulness of the body haven’t forgotten freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.622:1.1: “Mendicants, those who haven’t cultivated mindfulness of the body haven’t cultivated freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.622:1.2: Those who have cultivated mindfulness of the body have cultivated freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.623:1.1: “Mendicants, those who haven’t developed mindfulness of the body haven’t developed freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.623:1.2: Those who have developed mindfulness of the body have developed freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.624:1.1: “Mendicants, those who haven’t made much of mindfulness of the body haven’t made much of the deathless.
translation_text.an1.624:1.2: Those who have made much of mindfulness of the body have made much of the deathless.”
translation_text.an1.625:1.1: “Mendicants, those who haven’t had insight into mindfulness of the body haven’t had insight into freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.625:1.2: Those who have had insight into mindfulness of the body have had insight into freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.626:1.1: “Mendicants, those who haven’t completely understood mindfulness of the body haven’t completely understood freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.626:1.2: Those who have completely understood mindfulness of the body have completely understood freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.627:1.1: “Mendicants, those who haven’t realized mindfulness of the body haven’t realized freedom from death.
translation_text.an1.627:1.2: Those who have realized mindfulness of the body have realized freedom from death.”
translation_text.an1.627:1.3: The thousand discourses of the Ones are completed.
translation_text.an1.627:2.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.an1.627:2.2: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what the Buddha said.
translation_text.an1.627:2.4: The Book of the Ones is finished.

variant_text.an1.627:1.3: (…) → (ekakanipātassa suttasahassaṁ samattaṁ.) (bj); (ekanipātassa suttasahassaṁ samattaṁ.) (sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an1.627:2.1: Idamavoca bhagavā … → etthantare pāṭho si, sya-all, km, pts1ed potthakesu natthi
